sutta: is an important suta also monks there are these 8 conditions 8 causes conducive to getting wisdom not yet gotten as to first things in holy living and when got into making it become more to its development so that it is made become made perfect what aid hearing among dwells near the teacher or some companion in the holy life whom he regards as teacher so that he is firmly established in conscientiousness fear of blame or a sense of remorse love and respect this is the first condition the first cause conducive to getting wisdom not yet gotten as to first things in holy living and when got into making it become more to its development so that it is made become made perfect so dwelling and so established from time to time he approaches his teachers questioning and inquiring of them thus sirs how is this what is the meaning of this to him those referencers reveal what is hidden explain the obscure and dispel doubt in many perplexing matters this is the second condition when he has heard dhamma he strives in his 2-fold seclusion that of the body and that of the mind this is the third condition he is virtuous and leaves restrained by the restraint of the party moka he is perfect in conduct and resort seeing danger in the smallest form he undertakes and trains himself in the preceptual training this is the fourth condition learned is he with memory retentive and well stored those doctrines lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle and lovely in the end which set forth in spirit and in letter and the holy life of purity perfect in its entirety even those are fully mastered by him resolved upon familiarized by speech bonded over in mind fully understood in theory this is the fifth condition living resolute he puts away all unwholesomeness and takes to wholesome conditions firm and energetic he shirks not the burden of wholesomeness this is the sixth condition moreover he frequents the order he is no gossip nor talker on childish matters either he speaks on dhamma himself or asks him of another nor does he neglect the aryan silence this is the seventh condition furthermore he abides in the contemplation of the rise and fall of the 5 kandas of attachment thinking such is form such is it's coming to be such is it's passing away so in respect of feelings perceptions volition and consciousness this is the eighth condition the eighth cause conducive to getting wisdom not yet gotten as to first things in holy living and when gotten to making it become more to its development so that it is made become made perfect presently his fellows in the holy life honor him thus this ravensword dwells near the teacher or some comrade in the holy life whom he regards as teacher and that is firmly established in conscience and there is firmly established in conscientiousness fear of blame love and respect surely this referencer is 1 through knowing knows and seeing sees verily this con this condition is conducive to the state of being dear and esteemed to becoming made become to reclusive and to singleness of mind in making become and so true for each of the other 7 conditions these monks are the 8 conditions the 8 causes conducive to getting wisdom not yet gotten as to first things in holy living and when gotten to making it become more to its development so that it is made become made perfect so this in this 8 things are important for a manga to get wisdom especially if it's a new monk the first 1 that he dwells he lives with a teacher so that he can establish or sense of conscience and fear of blame or a sense of remorse if he does anything wrong and also love for the teacher and respect for the teacher is the first condition the second 1 is since he has a teacher then he frequently asks anything that he does not understand that is in doubt he asked so that the teacher explains to him the meaning and the third 1 he strives in 2-fold seclusion that of body and and that of mind bodily situation is to be um physically secluded from other people either a person can live in a forest monastery or the person can live in any monastery but but to be a bit aloo from other people not to mix too much with other people or talk with other people [Music] but the highest form of bodily seclusion is to live alone from uh from from everybody here to live in a forest on a cave alone and then that would be the highest degree of bodily seclusion mental seclusion is to be secluded from the developments that means to practice meditation so that the mind attains 1 pointedness of mind so that the defilements the thinking etc are less likely to arise and then the third condition is his virtuousness and lives restrained by the pati moka the set of rules and precepts and after that he's learned he's learned in the dhamma the discourses of the buddha is well versed in with the discourses of the buddha another condition is his energetic energetic in um putting away unwholesome states of mind and cultivating wholesome states of mind and then he's no gossip he doesn't talk too much and either he talks about dhamma or he asks another to talk about dhamma and when there's there's no uh topic on dharma to be discussed and then he keeps the aryan silence and then he abides contemplating the 5 conducts of attachment the 5 aggregates uh form uh body feeling perception volition and consciousness uh noting their rising and passing away so these are the conditions conducive to getting wisdom the next suta is
